hooks.prompt: Plane-ops plugin is loaded. If this session will involve Plane (sprint, backlog, work item, cycle, epic, module, milestone, initiative, roadmap, standup, retro, estimate, board, ticket, task), run the `connector-bootstrap` skill to discover Plane tools BEFORE attempting any operation. Do not assume tools are missing — probe ToolSearch with at least 4 queries (plane, work_item, cycle, create_cycle, plus domain-specific terms) and match tools by action suffix. If multiple Plane instances are connected, ask the user which one to use. This is a silent reminder — do not mention it to the user unless they ask about Plane.

DESTRUCTIVE PLANE OPERATION DETECTED. Before proceeding with this delete call, verify with the user: (1) Show the user the exact resource being deleted (name, ID, and a one-line description of what it contains). (2) Explicitly state what will be lost and what will be unlinked but preserved — e.g., `delete_cycle` unlinks items but preserves them; `delete_project` is catastrophic; `delete_state` requires migrating items first. (3) Ask the user 'Proceed with the delete? (yes/no)' and WAIT for 'yes'. Only continue after explicit confirmation. If the user says no or is ambiguous, abort and offer a safer alternative (archive instead of delete, migrate state first, etc.). Never delete production data without this confirmation even if the user previously approved a related operation — each destructive call needs its own confirmation.
